task_completion.description: Navigate to the 'Gears' activity within GCompris.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris application window is open and displaying an activity involving gears, a motor (driver), and a red target gear.

task_completion.description: Move loose gears onto the main board.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Loose gears have been dragged from their starting inventory/sidebar and placed onto the main play area.

task_completion.description: Connect the driver gear to the red target gear using the loose gears.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The placed gears form a continuous, interlocking chain where the teeth of each gear touch the next, successfully bridging the gap between the motor gear and the red target gear.
Visual indicators that the mechanism is active and rotating, such as motion blur, rotation animations, a success prompt/overlay, or the red target gear lighting up/animating.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the completed mechanism to /home/ga/gears_success.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command (e.g., scrot, gnome-screenshot), a save dialog, or file manager showing the screenshot being saved to the exact path '/home/ga/gears_success.png'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GCompris GUI to solve the puzzle.
integrity.visual_evidence: Trajectory screenshots show the mouse cursor actively dragging and dropping gears within the GCompris application window.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the task by downloading or opening a pre-existing image of a solved gear puzzle.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screen shows the live GCompris application being manipulated, not an image viewer, web browser, or file previewer displaying a static image.

integrity.description: The final screenshot is genuinely captured from the agent's own solution.
integrity.visual_evidence: The saved screenshot matches the final state of the GCompris window as seen in the preceding trajectory steps, without signs of external image manipulation or copying.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected screenshot path is verified as /home/ga/gears_success.png. Note that the 'Gears' activity may be a custom or synthetic module within GCompris; evaluate success based strictly on the visual presence of a motor gear, a red target gear, and interlocking loose gears forming a functional chain. The minimum screenshot size is enforced at 5000 bytes.
